title: Build source-owned CodeWiki audit tool and /audit command
summary: Create the source-owned audit engine and single /audit [flags] user command with profile-scoped machine-readable evidence; scripts may wrap it but cannot own audit semantics.

goal.outcome: Users, gateways, and agents invoke the same source-owned audit engine for full or scoped audits, and audit results are machine-readable evidence.
acceptance: Audit engine lives in source-owned application/domain code, not scripts, and supports full and selected audit profiles.
Internal agent-callable audit tool runs full audit and selected profiles through the shared application API.
/audit command wraps the same engine and supports file-structure, security, alignment, package, changed, task, and layer flags.
Audit output includes profile, checked scopes, issues, evidence refs, and source/content fingerprints where available.
Tests cover default full audit and at least three scoped profiles.
non_goals: Do not make audits decide gateway verdicts directly.
Do not require full audit for every gateway call.
Do not place authoritative audit rules in scripts.

delta.desired: One source-owned audit substrate serves users, agents, generated state, and gateways; scripts are optional wrappers only.
delta.current: Audits are partly ad hoc and previous drift guards lived in scripts/check-architecture.mjs.
delta.closure: Audit tool/command exist with scoped profiles, tests, and no authoritative script-owned semantics.
